name: Jardín de La Alcarria — circular through the lavender fields of Brihuega
name_original: Jardín de La Alcarria — circular por los campos de lavanda de Brihuega
region_intro: Hiking around Brihuega offers diverse landscapes within the Tajuña Valley, characterized by lush vegetation and varied terrain. The region features extensive lavender fields, particularly vibrant in July, and views of the Buendía and Entrepeñas Reservoirs. Hikers can explore riverside paths, gentle hills, and areas like the Peñascos de los Frailes. The natural environment provides a range of trails for different skill levels. Best hiking trails around Brihuega The most popular hiking route is The Garduño de Cela — circular through Durón, Budia and the Olivar, a 7.2 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the rural landscape around Durón, Budia, and the Olivar. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peñascos de los Frailes — circular desde Las Inviernas, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.8 km) path. This trail offers views of rock formations and the surrounding countryside near Las Inviernas. Local hikers also love the Civica and Civica waterfall - circular along the Tajuña river, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail leading through the Tajuña river valley, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Hiking around Brihuega is defined by the Tajuña Valley, extensive lavender fields, and scenic reservoirs. The network offers options for easy walks to more moderate routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Brihuega are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from 15 reviews. More than 77 hikers have used komoot to explore Brihuega's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Brihuega?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails in the Brihuega area, offering a variety of experiences. These include 5 easy routes and 15 moderate routes, ensuring options for different fitness levels.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Brihuega?
A: The best time to hike in Brihuega is generally during late spring and early summer, especially in July, when the extensive lavender fields transform the landscape into a 'purple paradise'. This offers a unique sensory experience and beautiful vistas.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or significant water features?
A: Yes, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027268114/civica-and-civica-waterfall-circular-along-the-tajuna-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Civica and Civica waterfall - circular along the Tajuña river&lt;/a&gt;. This 10.5 km circular trail follows the Tajuña river, leading to the Civica waterfall and offering scenic views of the valley.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Brihuega?
A: Many of the trails around Brihuega are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030489046/the-garduno-de-cela-circular-through-duron-budia-and-the-olivar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garduño de Cela&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate circular route exploring the rural landscape, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027253632/jardin-de-la-alcarria-circular-through-the-lavender-fields-of-brihuega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jardín de La Alcarria&lt;/a&gt; offers a circular walk through the lavender fields.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly hiking options in Brihuega?
A: Yes, Brihuega offers 5 easy routes suitable for families or those looking for a more relaxed walk. The diverse terrain includes riverside paths and gentle hills, making it accessible for various skill levels. Many paths are well-maintained and suitable for a leisurely stroll.

Q: What natural landmarks or attractions can I see while hiking in the region?
A: Hikers can enjoy several natural highlights. The Tajuña Valley provides lush vegetation, while the lavender fields are a major draw in July. You can also find stunning views of the Buendía and Entrepeñas Reservoirs, and explore areas like the Peñascos de los Frailes, known for its rock formations.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Brihuega?
A: The hiking trails in Brihuega are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from 15 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the Tajuña Valley to the vibrant lavender fields, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various abilities.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, Brihuega itself is rich in history. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4440570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Piedra Bermeja&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views, or discover the unique Arab Caves, a network of hidden tunnels beneath the town. These attractions provide a cultural complement to your outdoor adventures.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Brihuega suitable for dogs?
A: Generally, most hiking trails in natural areas like Brihuega are dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. It's always recommended to carry water for your pet and clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Brihuega?
A: The terrain around Brihuega is varied, ranging from lush riverside paths along the Tajuña river to gentle hills and open fields, particularly around the lavender plantations. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027290918/penascos-de-los-frailes-circular-desde-las-inviernas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñascos de los Frailes&lt;/a&gt; offer views of unique rock formations and surrounding countryside.

Q: Are there longer, more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: While most routes in the immediate Brihuega area are moderate, for more adventurous hikers, the region offers access to more challenging ascents like Pico Ocejón (2,049 meters) in the wider area, which provides impressive panoramic views. Within Brihuega, the 15 moderate routes offer good distances and elevation gains.
tips:
Get to know the Alcarria Garden, its lavender fields and its monuments, just an hour from Madrid.
Don't miss the Lavender fields and the Arab Caves at this time.

The best time to visit the lavender fields of Brihuega is in July, from the middle to the end of the month, especially at the end of the second half of July, which are the most colorful weeks and when the lavender flower is in its maximum bloom. and splendor.

waypoints.name: Lavender Fields and Arab Caves of Brihuega
